stdout:
Usage: gaia search lkm [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

 Search Bohrium LKM paper knowledge, reasoning chains, and workflows.

╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --help          Show this message and exit.                                  │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ docs       Print LKM API documentation links.                                │
│ knowledge  Search LKM paper knowledge items (POST /search).                  │
│ reasoning  Search reasoning chains, or fetch them for one claim with         │
│            --claim-id.                                                       │
│ nodes      Batch-fetch LKM node record detail.                               │
│ package    Fetch an LKM paper package candidate (POST /papers/graph).        │
│ feedback   Submit LKM service/data feedback (POST /feedback).                │
│ auth       Manage the LKM access key (login / status / logout / rotate).     │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

 Auth: every call needs a Bohrium access key. Run `gaia search lkm auth login`
 to set one up (or set GAIA_LKM_ACCESS_KEY / LKM_ACCESS_KEY).
 LKM (Large Knowledge Model) is Bohrium's agent-ready paper search engine for
 grounding scientific claims, inspecting reasoning chains, and resolving source
 papers; it is not Gaia's internal IR or a generic graph API.
 Search surfaces: `knowledge` retrieves paper knowledge items, including
 conclusion claims, weak-point / highlight claims, problems, and open
 questions. `reasoning` retrieves reasoning chains and workflows. `package`
 fetches the per-paper graph that `gaia pkg add` can materialize as a local
 dependency. `feedback` reports LKM service/data issues.
 Configured indexes: bohrium (default). Set GAIA_LKM_INDEX_<NAME>_URL to add a
 named LKM index.
 API docs: https://s.apifox.cn/<UUID4> Endpoint
 links: gaia search lkm docs
 Exit codes: 0 ok / 1 business error / 2 transport / 3 no key / 4 bad args.
 Note: the `score` field returned by `knowledge` / `reasoning` is a retrieval
 ranking signal, not a probability — do not pass it to Gaia priors.
